Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First, lets list all activities per case and see which ones recur within the same case. Case C001: - Initiate Request - Verify Documents (appears twice: at 09:20 and 10:30) - Call Customer - Submit Additional - Approve Request Case C002: - Initiate Request - Verify Documents (once) - Call Customer (appears twice: at 09:00 and 09:50) - Adjust Account - Approve Request Case C003: - Initiate Request - Verify Documents (appears twice: at 11:25 and 12:20) - Call Customer - Adjust Account - Approve Request So, the activities that recur within the same case are: - Verify Documents in C001 and C003 (twice each) - Call Customer in C002 (twice)

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets examine each recurrence: 1. Verify Documents in C001: - First occurrence: Agent checks identity documents. (09:20) - Second occurrence: Agent re-checks documents now that ID scan is provided. (10:30) So, the first is an initial verification, the second is a re-verification after additional documents are submitted. 2. Verify Documents in C003: - First occurrence: Physical verification of ID. (11:25) - Second occurrence: Re-verify docs after account adjustment. (12:20) Again, first is initial verification, second is re-verification after an adjustment. 3. Call Customer in C002: - First occurrence: Outbound call to clarify account discrepancy. (09:00) - Second occurrence: Follow-up call to confirm adjustments made. (09:50) So, the first call is for clarification, the second is a follow-up call.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The instruction: Identify activities that appear multiple times within the same case so we only need to disambiguate within a case. Therefore, we will: - In C001, rename the two Verify Documents activities to distinguish initial and re-verification. - In C002, rename the two Call Customer activities to distinguish clarification and follow-up. - In C003, rename the two Verify Documents activities to distinguish initial and re-verification.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Now, we need to choose appropriate new names. Lets look at the additional context: For C001: - First Verify Documents: Agent checks identity documents. -> We can call it Initial Document Verification or Identity Verification. - Second Verify Documents: Agent re-checks documents now that ID scan is provided. -> We can call it Document Re-verification or Re-verification after Additional Document. For C003: ... For C002 Call Customer: - First: Outbound call to clarify account discrepancy. -> Clarification Call or Discrepancy Clarification Call. - Second: Follow-up call to confirm adjustments made. -> Follow-up Call or Confirmation Call. Lets propose new names: For C001: - First Verify Documents -> Initial Document Verification - Second Verify Documents -> Document Re-verification For C002: - First Call Customer -> Clarification Call - Second Call Customer -> Follow-up Call For C003: - First Verify Documents -> Initial Document Verification - Second Verify Documents -> Document Re-verification

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Now, note that Verify Documents also appears once in C002, but only once, so we dont need to disambiguate within that case. However, to maintain consistency across cases... But the task says rename the activities so that their names clearly reflect their contextual differences. This implies that we should rename based on the context within the case. ... Alternatively, we could use more specific names based on the context. ... I think consistency is good. Lets use: - For first occurrence of Verify Documents in a case: Initial Document Verification - For second occurrence: Document Re-verification

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Well change only the Activity column for the rows that need disambiguation. ... Revised Event Log: [tables for C001, C002, C003 with renamed activities] Justification: For C001: - The first Verify Documents occurs right after the request initiation and involves checking identity documents. It is the initial verification step, so renamed to Initial Document Verification. - The second Verify Documents occurs after the customer has submitted additional documents (ID scan). It is a re-verification to ensure completeness, hence renamed to Document Re-verification. For C002: ... For C003: ... These renamings disambiguate the recurring activities within each case, making the process flow clearer.
